title: Squeeze Loop (SL) — compact standardized representation
description: A machine-checkable encoding of a single squeeze loop. The load-bearing idea: every source of truth has a stable id, and each actor references sources by id in its bounds and barriers, so disjointness (C1), barrier consistency (C3) and endogeneity become set operations a tool can run.

id.description: Stable slug for the loop, e.g. 'my-loop'.

kind.description: base = produces a deliverable through a cast; monitor = squeezes another loop's soft outputs (sl-monitoring-sl); meta = an audit methodology with no 5-actor cast (sl-auditor).

description.description: One short paragraph: what the loop delivers and the dominant coherent-and-wrong it guards.

terrain.description: Terrain archetype(s): A transcription / B authored authority / C split planes.

monitors.description: For kind=monitor/meta: the id of the loop this one observes (null otherwise).

sources.description: The registry of every source of truth, artifact, oracle, rationale and terminus in the loop. This registry is what makes disjointness checkable.

actors.description: The rows of the compact table, one object per actor.

name.description: Human-readable name as it appears in the prose tables.

type.description: soft_authority = U-material (spec/standard/policy). executable_oracle = L-material (runnable, interpretation-free). artifact = a thing produced or under test (impl, contracts, tests). rationale = a producer's private reasoning (a judge must never read it). human_judgment = a terminus judgment, no executable oracle.

provenance.description: exogenous = independent of the artifact under test (gold). endogenous = read off the artifact under test (a disjointness violation at the bound level; a PASS against it is only self-consistency). authored = an upstream-authored U (terrain B). external_tool = a prover/checker/runtime. human = a human terminus. internal = produced inside this loop (set automatically by produced_by).

produced_by.description: actor id that creates this source inside the loop, or null if it enters from outside. Drives the self-certification check: no actor may judge against a source it produced.

executable.description: True only for sources whose verdict is mechanical and re-runnable.

bound.description: An (upper or lower) bound: prose + the set of source ids it rests on.

text.description: The bound as written in the compact table.

sources.description: ids into sources[] this bound reads from. EMPTY + absent_reason set => a terminus with no executable L.

executable.description: For L: is this bound a runnable oracle?

borrowed_from.description: For monitor/meta loops whose bound IS the observed loop's bound: the observed loop id.

absent_reason.description: If sources is empty: why (e.g. 'no executable oracle — that absence is why it is the terminus').

role.description: Canonical cast plus the meta/monitor roles. human_terminus / disjoint_base carry no executable L. monitor borrows the observed loop's bounds. sub_loop is a bridge row: an entire base loop collapsed to one row.

builds.description: The 'Builds' row — the deliverable this actor produces.

produces.description: source ids this actor authors (must match sources[].produced_by). Used by the self-certification check.

forbidden_moves.description: The role-crossing actions that would relieve this actor's own pressure.

must_not_see.description: source ids that must be ABSENT from this actor's context (physical barrier, not honorary). A judge runs an oracle over an artifact; it never reads the artifact in must_not_see.

invokes.description: For a borrowed/invoked actor (e.g. the skill sub-monitor = the sl-monitoring-sl Monitor row, invoked not re-derived).

expands_to.description: For a bridge row: the loop id this single row collapses (e.g. 'base-loop' inside its monitor). Its real cast lives in that loop's own document.

rationale.description: The 'at a glance' prose: why no actor can certify its own work.

load_bearing_barrier.description: The one separation the whole loop rests on.

catchability.description: C2 made explicit: for each actor's characteristic blind spot, the OTHER actor that catches it.

caught_by.description: actor id of the catcher (must differ from 'actor').

endogeneity_flags.description: Sources/actors where U is endogenous: a PASS there certifies self-consistency, not conformance. Empty = none.

terminus.description: For soft-side loops: who supplies the human/cross-provider close (the disjoint base). null for fully mechanical loops.
